---
title: "Figure S4 — bias and autocorrelation diagnostics, data level and inference level, across the 5 macro algorithms"
author: "Luciano Moffatt"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

<!--
Merged supplement to the main Figure 3 (figure_3_alt.Rmd): the FIRST-MOMENT (bias) and TEMPORAL
(autocorrelation) diagnostics, at BOTH the residual/data level and the score/inference level, for
all four fitted parameters (k_on, k_off, i, N_ch), across the 5 macro approximations
(NR, MNR, R, MR, IR; the data-file key "NMR" is displayed as "MNR"). The residual VARIANCE row is
NOT here: it is already the residual-variance row of the main Figure 3, so it would be redundant.

Reads the 5 ensemble CSVs figures/data/figure_3_time_dlik_{NR,NMR,R,MR,IR}.csv (each > 1 GB, 1000
sims; cell N_ch=100, Current_Noise=1e-4 [matches Fig 2], interval=0.1 tau) ONCE each. From each
file two primitives are kept:
  r_std  = (patch_current - y_mean)/sqrt(y_var)          (component_path=="r_std", calculus=="primitive")
  s_t,k  = d logL_t / d theta_k, k in {k_on,k_off,i,N_ch} (component_path=="logL", calculus=="derivative",
           param_index in {0,1,2,5})
Each parameter/primitive appears twice in the dump (a blank-segment copy + a segment_index=0 copy,
identical values); the duplicate is collapsed by mean over the grouping keys, else the score sums
double. Time axis in milliseconds (step_middle * 1000).

Layout: header strip + 10 diagnostic rows x 5 approximation columns, in two blocks grouped by
x-domain so the axes stay clean.

  BIAS block (x = TIME in ms, agonist shading, LINEAR signed y, green null at 0, grouped naive|recursive):
    A  residual bias           mean over recordings of r_std
    B  score bias  k_on        mean over recordings of s_t,k
    C  score bias  k_off
    D  score bias  i
    E  score bias  N_ch        <- bottom of the TIME block: x-numbers + single "time (ms)" title (col R)
  ACF block (x = LAG, no shading, LINEAR signed y, green null at 0, grouped):
    F  residual autocorrelation   mean of per-recording acf(r_std) vs lag
    G  score autocorrelation  k_on
    H  score autocorrelation  k_off
    I  score autocorrelation  i
    J  score autocorrelation  N_ch  <- bottom of the LAG block: x-numbers + single "lag" title (col R)

Rows A-E carry x-numbers only on E, one "time (ms)" title on E's centre (R) column; rows F-J carry
x-numbers only on J, one "lag" title on J's centre (R) column; every other row has no x-numbers.
Both diagnostics are SIGNED, so every panel is on a LINEAR y with 0 forced onto the ticks and a
green null line at 0. y-scale is per group (naive NR/MNR | recursive R/MR/IR), per row: the naive
gradient/ACF blows up and on one shared scale would crush the recursive trio near 0. Bands are
mean +/- 1.96 SE over recordings (bias) or per lag (ACF).

The story: read a COLUMN (one approximation) down the bias rows, or down the ACF rows. Within each
block the residual and score diagnostics look alike, and the pattern is the same for every
parameter (naive biased + temporally correlated; recursive ~ 0). k_off in the main figures is
representative, and the data-level and inference-level miscalibrations are the same phenomenon.

RENDER: reads > 5 GB across the 5 CSVs; too heavy for a laptop. Run only where figures/data/
resolves and there is memory to spare.
-->

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(data.table)
library(patchwork)
ALGOS <- c("NR", "NMR", "R", "MR", "IR")                             # NMR = data-file key
ALAB  <- c(NR = "NR", NMR = "MNR", R = "R", MR = "MR", IR = "IR")    # display: show NMR as MNR
FULL  <- c(NR = "Non-Recursive", NMR = "Mean Non-Recursive", R = "Recursive",
           MR = "Mean Recursive", IR = "Interval Recursive")
PIDX  <- c(0, 1, 2, 5)                                               # k_on, k_off, i, N_ch
PLAB  <- c("k[on]", "k[off]", "i", "N[ch]")                          # plotmath parameter labels
```

```{r read}
# each CSV once: the standardized residual r_std AND the per-interval score of the 4 parameters,
# both deduped over the writer's x2 identical copies (mean over the grouping keys), plus the timing.
read_one <- function(a) {
  dt <- fread(sprintf("../data/figure_3_time_dlik_%s.csv", a), skip = 1, showProgress = FALSE)
  rr <- dt[component_path == "r_std" & calculus == "primitive" & !is.na(sample_index),
           .(r_std = mean(value)), by = .(simulation_index, sample_index)]                # dedup x2
  sc <- dt[component_path == "logL" & calculus == "derivative" &
           param_index %in% PIDX & !is.na(sample_index),
           .(s = mean(value)), by = .(simulation_index, sample_index, param_index)]        # dedup x2
  ti <- unique(dt[!is.na(patch_current), .(sample_index, t = step_middle * 1000, agonist)])# step_middle s -> ms
  ti <- ti[, .(t = first(t), agonist = first(agonist)), by = sample_index]                 # one row / sample
  rm(dt); gc(verbose = FALSE)
  rr <- merge(rr, ti[, .(sample_index, t)], by = "sample_index")
  list(resid = rr[is.finite(r_std)][, algo := a],
       sco   = sc[is.finite(s)][, algo := a],
       tm    = ti)
}
raw  <- lapply(ALGOS, read_one)
RR   <- rbindlist(lapply(raw, `[[`, "resid")); RR[, algo := factor(algo, levels = ALGOS)]
SC   <- rbindlist(lapply(raw, `[[`, "sco"));   SC[, algo := factor(algo, levels = ALGOS)]
tmap <- raw[[1]]$tm
setorder(RR, algo, simulation_index, sample_index)
setorder(SC, algo, param_index, simulation_index, sample_index)   # ACF needs sample order
n_samp <- uniqueN(RR$sample_index)
```

```{r bias-stats}
# residual bias: mean over recordings of r_std per (algo, step); ~ 0 for a calibrated prediction.
RB <- RR[, .(t = first(t), n = .N, m = mean(r_std), se = sd(r_std) / sqrt(.N)),
         by = .(algo, sample_index)]
RB[, algo := factor(algo, levels = ALGOS)]
# score bias: mean over recordings of s_t,k per (algo, param, step); ~ 0 for a martingale-difference
# (well-specified) score at every step. A localised departure marks where mis-specification biases
# the gradient.
SB <- SC[, .(n = .N, m = mean(s), se = sd(s) / sqrt(.N)),
         by = .(algo, param_index, sample_index)]
SB <- merge(SB, tmap[, .(sample_index, t)], by = "sample_index")
SB[, `:=`(algo  = factor(algo, levels = ALGOS),
          param = factor(PLAB[match(param_index, PIDX)], levels = PLAB))]
```

```{r acf-stats}
# per-recording ACF of the ordered series, then mean over recordings +/- 1.96 SE per lag. A white
# (uncorrelated) residual/score has ACF ~ 0 at lags >= 1; residual memory -> the F != J that
# distorts the Fisher CIs of Figure 2.
LAGMAX  <- min(25, n_samp - 2)
acf_one <- function(x) as.numeric(acf(x, lag.max = LAGMAX, plot = FALSE)$acf)
# residual autocorrelation (data level)
RA <- as_tibble(RR) %>%
  group_by(algo, simulation_index) %>%
  summarise(a = list(acf_one(r_std)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(algo) %>% summarise(M = list(do.call(rbind, a)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(d = map(M, ~ tibble(lag = 0:LAGMAX, acf = colMeans(.x),
                             se = apply(.x, 2, sd) / sqrt(nrow(.x))))) %>%
  select(algo, d) %>% unnest(d) %>% filter(lag >= 1)
RA <- as.data.table(RA); RA[, algo := factor(algo, levels = ALGOS)]
# score autocorrelation (inference level), per parameter
SA <- as_tibble(SC) %>%
  group_by(algo, param_index, simulation_index) %>%
  summarise(a = list(acf_one(s)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(algo, param_index) %>%
  summarise(M = list(do.call(rbind, a)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(d = map(M, ~ tibble(lag = 0:LAGMAX, acf = colMeans(.x),
                             se = apply(.x, 2, sd) / sqrt(nrow(.x))))) %>%
  select(algo, param_index, d) %>% unnest(d) %>% filter(lag >= 1)
SA <- as.data.table(SA)
SA[, `:=`(algo  = factor(algo, levels = ALGOS),
          param = factor(PLAB[match(param_index, PIDX)], levels = PLAB))]
```

```{r panels, fig.width = 7.0, fig.height = 14.0}
COL   <- "#0072B2"; NUL <- "#009E73"                     # house blue; green null line
naive <- c("NR", "NMR"); recg <- c("R", "MR", "IR")      # keys (NMR displayed as MNR)

# per-group y-range (naive | recursive), padded: the naive gradient/ACF blows up and on one
# shared scale would crush the recursive trio's ~0, so each block gets its own axis.
grange <- function(lo, hi, algo, pad = .08) {
  g <- tibble(lo, hi, grp = ifelse(algo %in% naive, 1L, 2L)) %>% group_by(grp) %>%
    summarise(lo = min(lo), hi = max(hi), .groups = "drop") %>%
    mutate(pd = (hi - lo) * pad, lo = lo - pd, hi = hi + pd)
  list(naive = c(g$lo[g$grp == 1], g$hi[g$grp == 1]), rec = c(g$lo[g$grp == 2], g$hi[g$grp == 2]))
}

# column-header strip cell (approximations on top, ONCE); ALAB shows the NMR key as MNR
colhead <- function(k) ggplot() + theme_void() + ggtitle(ALAB[[k]], subtitle = FULL[[k]]) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = .5, size = 9), plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = .5, size = 7))

# decorate a panel. Signed quantities only -> LINEAR y, 0 forced onto the ticks. y-NUMBERS on NR
# (+ its quantity title) and, for the grouped rows, on R (the recursive block's leftmost). x-NUMBERS
# on every bottom-of-block panel, but the x TITLE only ONCE, on the centre column (R).
deco <- function(p, col, split, xr, yl, yq, ttl = NULL, tag = NULL, bottom = FALSE, xlab = NULL) {
  ylim <- if (is.list(yl)) (if (col %in% naive) yl$naive else yl$rec) else yl
  brk  <- pretty(ylim); brk <- sort(unique(c(0, brk))); brk <- brk[brk >= ylim[1] & brk <= ylim[2]]
  p <- p + scale_y_continuous(breaks = brk) + coord_cartesian(xlim = xr, ylim = ylim)
  if (col == "NR") p <- p + ylab(yq) + theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8))
  else if (col == "R" && split) p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())            # numbers, no title
  else p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
  if (bottom) {
    if (col == "R") p <- p + xlab(xlab) + theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8))
    else p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
  } else p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank())
  if (!is.null(tag)) p <- p + labs(tag = tag)
  if (!is.null(ttl)) p <- p + ggtitle(ttl) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0, size = 8.5, face = "plain", margin = margin(b = 1)))
  p + theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), plot.margin = margin(1.5, 3, 1.5, 3))
}
base <- function() theme_bw(base_size = 8)

tr   <- range(c(RB$t, SB$t)); lr <- range(c(1, RA$lag, SA$lag))
agon <- tmap[agonist > 0]; x0 <- min(agon$t); x1 <- max(agon$t)   # agonist window (ms)
# full-panel agonist band for the TIME (bias) rows only; linear panels need a negative floor.
mkshade <- function() annotate("rect", xmin = x0, xmax = x1, ymin = -1e10, ymax = 1e10,
                               fill = "grey85", alpha = .5)

# left row titles (plotmath) for the score rows, one per parameter x block
TTL_SB <- list(expression(plain("score bias:") ~ k[on]),  expression(plain("score bias:") ~ k[off]),
               expression(plain("score bias:") ~ i),       expression(plain("score bias:") ~ N[ch]))
TTL_SA <- list(expression(plain("score autocorrelation:") ~ k[on]),
               expression(plain("score autocorrelation:") ~ k[off]),
               expression(plain("score autocorrelation:") ~ i),
               expression(plain("score autocorrelation:") ~ N[ch]))

# a bias row (one diagnostic, 5 approximation columns): mean over recordings +/- 1.96 SE over TIME,
# agonist shading, GROUPED naive|recursive scale, linear signed y.
bias_row <- function(d_all, ttl, tag, yq, bottom) {
  yl <- grange(d_all$m - 1.96 * d_all$se, d_all$m + 1.96 * d_all$se, as.character(d_all$algo))
  lapply(ALGOS, function(a) {
    d <- d_all[algo == a]
    g <- ggplot(d, aes(t, m)) + mkshade() + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = NUL, linewidth = .4) +
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = m - 1.96 * se, ymax = m + 1.96 * se), fill = COL, alpha = .25) +
      geom_line(colour = COL, linewidth = .4) + base()
    deco(g, a, TRUE, tr, yl, yq,
         ttl = if (a == "NR") ttl else NULL, tag = if (a == "NR") tag else NULL,
         bottom = bottom, xlab = if (bottom) "time (ms)" else NULL)
  })
}

# an ACF row (one diagnostic, 5 approximation columns): mean over recordings +/- 1.96 SE per LAG,
# no shading (x is lag), GROUPED naive|recursive scale, linear signed y.
acf_row <- function(d_all, ttl, tag, bottom) {
  yl <- grange(d_all$acf - 1.96 * d_all$se, d_all$acf + 1.96 * d_all$se, as.character(d_all$algo))
  lapply(ALGOS, function(a) {
    d <- d_all[algo == a]
    g <- ggplot(d, aes(lag, acf)) + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = NUL, linewidth = .4) +
      geom_linerange(aes(ymin = acf - 1.96 * se, ymax = acf + 1.96 * se), colour = COL, linewidth = .35) +
      geom_point(colour = COL, size = .25) + base()
    deco(g, a, TRUE, lr, yl, "ACF",
         ttl = if (a == "NR") ttl else NULL, tag = if (a == "NR") tag else NULL,
         bottom = bottom, xlab = if (bottom) "lag" else NULL)
  })
}

# header strip + BIAS block (A residual, B-E score k_on/k_off/i/N_ch) + ACF block (F residual,
# G-J score k_on/k_off/i/N_ch). E is the bottom of the TIME block; J is the bottom of the LAG block.
# TWO separate 5-row figures from the single data read: a BIAS figure and an ACF figure, each
# combining the residual (data-level) row with the four score (inference-level) rows. Each is
# re-lettered A-E in its own right.
gS4_bias <- wrap_plots(c(
  lapply(ALGOS, colhead),
  bias_row(RB,                    "residual bias",             "A", expression(bar(r)[std]), FALSE),
  bias_row(SB[param == PLAB[1]],  TTL_SB[[1]],                 "B", expression(bar(s)[t]),   FALSE),
  bias_row(SB[param == PLAB[2]],  TTL_SB[[2]],                 "C", expression(bar(s)[t]),   FALSE),
  bias_row(SB[param == PLAB[3]],  TTL_SB[[3]],                 "D", expression(bar(s)[t]),   FALSE),
  bias_row(SB[param == PLAB[4]],  TTL_SB[[4]],                 "E", expression(bar(s)[t]),   TRUE)),
  ncol = 5, heights = c(0.10, rep(1, 5)))
gS4_bias
ggsave("Figure_S4.pdf", gS4_bias, width = 7.0, height = 7.5)

gS4_acf <- wrap_plots(c(
  lapply(ALGOS, colhead),
  acf_row (RA,                    "residual autocorrelation",  "A", FALSE),
  acf_row (SA[param == PLAB[1]],  TTL_SA[[1]],                 "B", FALSE),
  acf_row (SA[param == PLAB[2]],  TTL_SA[[2]],                 "C", FALSE),
  acf_row (SA[param == PLAB[3]],  TTL_SA[[3]],                 "D", FALSE),
  acf_row (SA[param == PLAB[4]],  TTL_SA[[4]],                 "E", TRUE)),
  ncol = 5, heights = c(0.10, rep(1, 5)))
gS4_acf
ggsave("Figure_S5.pdf", gS4_acf, width = 7.0, height = 7.5)
```

```{r summary-table}
# numeric backing: mean |residual/score bias| and lag-1 ACF per algorithm (and parameter), the
# quantities the eye reads off the panels.
rb_tab <- RB[, .(diagnostic = "residual", param = "-",
                 max_abs_bias = round(max(abs(m), na.rm = TRUE), 3)), by = algo]
sb_tab <- SB[, .(diagnostic = "score",
                 max_abs_bias = round(max(abs(m), na.rm = TRUE), 3)), by = .(algo, param)]
ra_tab <- RA[lag == 1, .(diagnostic = "residual", param = "-", acf_lag1 = round(acf, 3)), by = algo]
sa_tab <- SA[lag == 1, .(diagnostic = "score", acf_lag1 = round(acf, 3)), by = .(algo, param)]
list(
  bias = rbind(rb_tab, sb_tab[, .(algo, diagnostic, param, max_abs_bias)], use.names = TRUE),
  acf  = rbind(ra_tab, sa_tab[, .(algo, diagnostic, param, acf_lag1)],     use.names = TRUE)
)
```
